title: Pinarayi Vijayan alleges Finance Bill passed in violation of Assembly rules to benefit private liquor firms
content: Opposition Leader accuses Chief Minister V.D. Satheesan of showing ‘unnecessary haste and stubbornness to provide tax cuts’ to liquor companies

title: Kerala High Court asks Law Secretary and Cabinet Secretary to appear unless progress is made on draft anti-ragging amendment Bill
content: Court observes that the matter has been pending for more than a year.  Upon its request, Election Commission of India had approved a Cabinet meeting to finalise draft Kerala Prohibition of Ragging (Amendment) Bill during Kerala Assembly elections. After formation of new State government, three weeks were given to take steps on draft Bill on June 10

title: Monsoon tracker LIVE: Man dies after falling into manhole amid heavy rains in Mumbai
content: The incident occurred in the Chandivali area, where waterlogging apparently concealed the uncovered manhole

title: Seven killed as boulder collapses at stone quarry on outskirts of Bengaluru, death toll likely to rise
content: Police said several labourers were reported missing, raising fears that the death toll could increase

title: Sabarimala gold loss: Former TDB president P.S. Prasanth denies role
content: Prasanth says ‘the only alleged lapse by certain officials’ was failing to inform Kerala High Court before sending gold-clad plates to Chennai for replating works in 2025

title: Foreign medical graduates allowed to take up internship in 36 non-teaching hospitals in Tamil Nadu
content: This has fulfilled a long-pending demand of hundreds of FMGs, who had passed the Foreign Medical Graduate Examination and were waiting for the mandatory CRMI

title: As Visakhapatnam rises skyward, groundwater sinks in Yendada-Madhurawada corridor
content: Experts warn that the city’s rapid vertical growth is outpacing water supply and natural recharge, leaving its fastest-growing suburbs more and more dependent on borewells and tankers

title: Tiruvallur ammonia gas leak: Death toll rises to 17
content: Of the 83 persons who were affected in the ammonia gas leak that occurred on June 21, 15 women are still hospitalised — one on ventilator support.

title: Contract of 134 Kerala’s Kudumbashree staff terminated
content: Order extending contract of other employees till September 30 issued. However, the staff under DAY-NULM project have not got any such relief

title: ‘Jana Nayagan’ leak case: 1.2 crore people watched Vijay-starrer online, Chennai police tell Madras High Court
content: Justice C. Kumarappan dismisses the bail petitions filed by two of the 21 accused after being told that they have been involved in a serious crime involving online leak of an uncertified movie

title: Congress-BRS confrontation escalates into showdown in Hyderabad; alleged scam at Telangana’s Gurukuls under spotlight
content: Leaders challenged each other to public debates over allegations of irregularities in the procurement of uniforms, shoes and school bags for students of Telangana’s residential educational institutions (Gurkulams)

title: Rules governing crèches in India | Explained
content: The heart-wrenching incident of caregivers abusing toddlers at the ‘Little Buds Day Care’ on the Capgemini campus has sparked outrage and raised serious questions about the safety measures needed in childcare facilities

title: Raja Raghuvanshi murder: Meghalaya police move Supreme Court against Sonam Raghuvanshi’s bail
content: The Meghalaya High Court, on June 29, had upheld an order of a Shillong trial court granting bail to Ms. Raghuvanshi on the ground that the police had failed to effectively communicate the grounds of her arrest

title: Bihar workers among 8 killed as massive rock collapse hits quarry near Bengaluru
content: At least eight workers were killed after a massive rock collapse at a stone quarry near Bengaluru. Several of the victims were daily-wage labourers from Bihar. Rescue teams are continuing operations while authorities investigate possible safety violations and negligence.

title: India, Japan unveil transformative agenda to boost ties
content: The Prime Minister said India and Japan have also taken several important decisions in the field of energy security

title: Keep me away from all this, I'm an artist: Diljit on CJP protests at Delhi’s Jantar Mantar
content: Everything in this world can never be right... So, those who are protesting, congratulations to them, and for whom the protest is happening, congratulations to them also, Diljit said

title: Submarine cable system project linking India, Malaysia, Singapore unveiled
content: Microsoft, Lightstorm, Singtel and Tata Communications consortium behind the proposed more than 3,600 km facility

title: Police take Siya Goyal to Pune spot where she allegedly rehearsed fiancé’s murder
content: The police recovered the clothes allegedly worn by Siya on June 18, the day of the incident, during a search at her residence in the Market Yard area of Pune

title: Karnataka CM raising Mekedatu dam issue for political gain: Anbumani Ramadoss
content: Demanding all MPs and MLAs to meet the Prime Minister and Jal Shakti Minister opposing the Mekedatu project, he said that continuous pressure should be given and many cases should be filed against this project.

title: ‘Methyl Isocyanate of Law’: Supreme Court quashes verdict based on AI-generated precedents
content: Supreme Court rejects NCLT verdict, condemning reliance on AI-generated fake precedents, emphasising zero tolerance for such practices

title: Adani Enterprises, UAE’s IHC to invest ₹1.08 lakh crore in Odisha aluminium project
content: The proposed investment is expected to be Odisha’s largest Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) proposal and India’s largest foreign direct investment in the metallurgy sector, government of Odisha officials said

title: Kerala HC refuses to extend transit bail granted to actor-husband of viral Kumbh Mela woman in kidnapping case
content: HC was informed by the State of Madhya Pradesh that anticipatory bail plea moved by Farmaan Khan had been dismissed by a Special Judge under POCSO Act in Mandleshwar, Madhya Pradesh

title: Telangana CM Revanth to visit Midjil, where his political journey began two decades ago
content: The visit is being billed not as a routine government programme but as a thanksgiving gesture to the people who first elected him and launched a political journey

title: ‘MAVIGUN’ is YSRCP’s election plank in 2029, says Jagan
content: We will project the proposal as an alternative development model and seek people’s mandate as the Amaravati project has become a conduit for corruption, says the YSRCP president

title: Former AIADMK Minister C. Vijayabaskar, senior leaders join TVK
content: Former Transport Minister M.R. Vijayabhaskar also joins party as exodus from the Dravidian major continues

title: Kerala govt begins examining Adani proposal to sell 49% stake in Vizhinjam port to MSC Group
content: Announcement by Adani Ports and Special Economic Zone Limited has sparked a controversy in Kerala, with Chief Minister V.D. Satheesan claiming Adani Group had not communicated its decision to State govt and that its approval is mandatory for such a move

title: Andhra Pradesh to set up task force against social media abuse, says Pawan Kalyan
content: The Deputy Chief Minister said a cell would be set up in his office to take complaints of online abuse and act under the BNS and the IT Act

title: The Hindu Group’s Chairman, Director call on Tamil Nadu CM Vijay
content: Chairman N. Murali congratulated the Chief Minister on the TVK’s historic victory in the 2026 Assembly elections

title: Actor Ansiba Hassan approaches court seeking FIR against actor Lakshmipriya, two others
content: Ansiba had filed a complaint against Lakshmipriya and the two others before SHO of  Palarivattom police. She had approached the court, alleging that the police did not take effective action on her complaint

title: How does WhatsApp’s proposed username feature compare to Telegram’s? | Explained
content: Government says the feature could ‘facilitate impersonation and identity spoofing, including impersonation by permitting the adoption of usernames closely resembling those of genuine persons or institutions’

title: Demography panel to study population changes post 2011 Census, prepares detailed questionnaire for States
content: The High-Level Committee on Demographic Changes will seek details of population changes and increase in settlements since the 2011 Census; it will also ask the ECI for data on SIR deletions

title: Three fans of actor Darshan held for threatening witness in Renukaswamy murder case
content: Police said one of the accused is the owner of the SUV that was allegedly used to transport Renukaswamy’s body from a shed in R.R. Nagar to a stormwater drain at Kamakshipalya, and also a witness in the murder case

title: A terrorist, is a terrorist: India calls for collective action to root out murderous ideology
content: Irrespective of any grievance, political cause or strategic calculation, terrorism in all its forms and manifestations must be condemned unequivocally, says India’s Permanent Representative to the UN Ambassador Harish Parvathaneni.

title: How is Apple affected by a data breach at Tata Electronics? | Explained
content: Tata is reportedly beefing up its security measures and organising a forensic audit after a cybersecurity incident involving data leaks related to clients Apple and Tesla

title: AGICL invites architectural firms to empanel for nearly 100 Amaravati capital projects
content: The document states that Amaravati is being developed as a world-class capital city, with nearly 100 infrastructure projects worth over ₹56,000 crore already under implementation

title: ‘Jana Nayagan’ gets CBFC nod after long delay, official says
content: The film’s release, originally slated for January, may finally be released at some point following the issue of a certificate in the coming days

title: Over 1,000 apply for Jana Sena posts in Vizianagaram ahead of local body polls
content: Ahead of the municipal and local body elections, the party has invited applications for committee posts across the district; most of the 1,000-plus applicants are aged 25 to 45

title: The case for equitable development in Tamil Nadu should apply to airport too: Poovulagin Nanbargal
content: Supporters of the Parandur airport argue that if Chennai does not get a second airport by 2028, the city and its surrounding industrial regions may lose future growth opportunities; the question, however, is whether such growth should be concentrated only around Chennai

title: Shortage of leukocyte filter sets continue to haunt thalassemia patients in Kozhikode
content: The supply of leukocyte filter sets under the ‘Ashadhara’ scheme was stopped last year after patients complained of adverse reactions

title: Why charge sheet in Pandit Sarla Bhat’s killing a milestone for J&K police? | Explained
content: The killing of the 27-year-old Pandit nurse was among the first cases of targeted killing of Kashmiri Pandits in the Valley following the armed insurrection in 1989; the filing of the charge sheet is seen by the family and the entire Pandit community as a step towards justice

title: Madras High Court dismisses ’Savukku’ Shankar’s plea for a CBI probe against former Chennai Police Commissioner Arun
content: Chief Justice Sushrut Arvind Dharmadhikari and Justice G. Arul Murugan say court’s jurisdiction must not be abused by individuals who pretend to fight for justice while acting on personal or hidden motives

title: Bombay civil court dismisses Gurbir Singh's plea against Samar Khadas, imposes costs
content: The dispute originated from a meeting held at the club premises, where Mr. Singh hosted a gathering that included individuals accused in the Bhima Koregaon case and facing prosecution under the UAPA

title: YSRCP claims Rayalaseema Steel Plant as its work, cadres stopped at Kadapa site
content: The delegation, led by former Minister Sake Sailajanath, tried to march to the site two days before its construction begins; the party says the project is its legacy

title: GST at 9 — Revenues up 14% in June 2026 but dependence on imports increased, domestic sales lagged
content: As GST marked nine years of implementation on July 1, tax experts point to several aspects — such as input tax credits, dispute resolution, multiple registrations, and the inverted duty structure — as issues that still need to be addressed

title: Arunachal clan launches drive to conserve endangered ray-finned fish, sustain tradition
content: Members of the Sangno clan translocate 52 fingerlings of the Schizothorax pelzami from one stream in East Kameng district to another, where they will be relatively safer from the predatory mahseer

title: Museum, water sports, resorts planned at Polavaram, says Nimmala Ramanaidu
content: The government is evaluating conceptual designs prepared by a Japanese consultancy for developing a tourism corridor from the Polavaram project to Bhadrachalam, via the Papikonda region, says Water Resources Minister Nimmala Ramanaidu

title: From rural Tamil Nadu to Wall Street: Chef Vijay Kumar on ringing the Nasdaq closing bell
content: In this exclusive essay for The Hindu, Vijay Kumar, the chef behind New York’s Michelin-starred Semma, reflects on his journey from a village near Natham in Tamil Nadu to global recognition, and what ringing the Nasdaq closing bell meant to him and the future of Tamil cuisine

title: Nellore will be a growth engine for wealth creation, says Chandrababu Naidu
content: BPCL is investing nearly ₹1 lakh crore in a refinery at Ramayapatnam, while large-scale industries are expected at IFFCO Kisan SEZ, says the Chief Minister

title: PM Modi, 'younger sister' Takaichi sign MoC including $10 billion investment, AI boost
content: India and Japan elevated their trade and strategic ties with a new Memorandum of Cooperation signed during the 16th Annual Summit in New Delhi. Prime Minister Modi welcomed his Japanese counterpart, highlighting shared priorities for a free and prosperous Indo-Pacific. The leaders discussed initiatives to boost Japanese investment in India, aiming for significant growth in capital and company presence, underscoring a strengthened partnership built on trust and shared values.

title: Navy foils piracy bid in Gulf of Aden on cargo ship carrying critical consignment for India
content: Indian Navy warship INS Trikand successfully thwarted a piracy attempt on merchant vessel MV Golden Arsenal in the Gulf of Aden. Responding swiftly to a distress call, the warship's presence caused suspected pirates to flee before they could board. Indian Marine Commandos later secured the vessel, which was carrying vital cargo for India. No crew injuries were reported.

title: Shreyas Iyer scripts history, becomes first Indian captain to...
content: Shreyas Iyer made history in Durham, becoming the first Indian captain to score a T20I half-century on English soil with a brilliant 68. His composed innings, crucial after an early collapse, helped India post 189/7. Though rain forced an abandonment, Iyer's return to form and record-breaking knock provides a significant boost for India ahead of the next match.

title: 'Wrote about ending his life': Pilot who hit Beijing's tallest building suffered from anxiety
content: A 66-year-old pilot named Liu faced a harrowing battle with anxiety and suicidal thoughts, culminating in a crash into the CITIC Group headquarters, the tallest building in Beijing. The aircraft, which took off after a routine flight, strayed off its intended path, resulting in injuries to 13 individuals. This alarming incident has ignited serious discussions about aviation security in a city known for its stringent controls.

title: This Apple privacy feature may have leaked emails for millions of iPhone, Mac users
content: Apple's Hide My Email feature has a bug that lets attackers unmask the real address behind any alias, and every tested case cracked in minutes. Researcher Tyler Murphy first flagged it in June 2025, but Apple's fixes never stuck. With over a billion paid subscriptions and two-thirds of iPhone owners on iCloud storage, millions of users relying on this "private" tool for anonymity may be exposed right now.

title: Man legally named 'Santa Claus' arrested in US child predator sting
content: A man legally named Santa Claus, formerly George Quigley, was arrested in Louisiana for allegedly soliciting a minor online. The 75-year-old, who adopted the Christmas persona, was apprehended during a sting operation after engaging in explicit online exchanges and sending nude photos. Police warn parents about the dangers of such online interactions, especially given the suspect's history of posing with children.

title: Why Zelenskyy wants to build US Patriot missiles in Ukraine after Russia's attack
content: In the wake of a substantial Russian airstrike, President Zelenskyy of Ukraine is appealing to the US for permission to manufacture Patriot air defense missiles domestically. This move comes as Kyiv strives to enhance its defenses following attacks that have left 13 dead and over 90 injured. By producing Patriots locally, Ukraine aims to secure a steady supply and lessen its dependence on external aid for sustained security.

title: Yes, we KANE: Harry saves England blushes with shot heard round the World Cup
content: Day 22 saw Harry Kane save England from Congolese chaos with a thunderbolt for the ages, Belgium summon one last Golden Generation miracle from the penalty spot, and the US survive both Bosnia and Herzegovina and VAR’s latest audition for villain of the tournament.

title: After Meta layoffs, Zuckerberg says firms should empower workers, not replace them
content: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg believes AI-driven job losses are exaggerated, advocating for tools that boost individual productivity. This comes as the tech giant laid off 8,000 employees, reassigning another 7,000 to AI projects, to offset significant investments in its AI "reboot." While Meta pours billions into AI infrastructure, Zuckerberg suggests a focus on human empowerment could create more jobs, a view contrasting with some industry leaders.

title: 60-year-old man dies after falling into uncovered manhole in Mumbai
content: A 60-year-old man, Aslam Isak Shaikh, has vanished after plunging into an open stormwater drain on Khairani Road in Mumbai's Sakinaka area. The incident occurred while civic workers were undertaking repairs on a drainage grill, leaving a manhole unattended. Authorities are actively conducting search operations and investigating the precise circumstances surrounding Shaikh's disappearance into the drain.

title: Rooftop solar failed to cut bills; consumer wins Rs 25,265 compensation
content: A solar installation firm has been ordered by a consumer commission to refund over Rs 25,000 to a homeowner. The company was found to have overcharged for net-metering and failed to deliver promised electricity generation from a rooftop solar system. The commission ruled the company vicariously liable for its agent's actions and awarded compensation for the shortfall in power and mental agony.

title: Cisco to roll out AI agents to entire workforce, says in-house AI is most efficient
content: Cisco is rolling out personalized AI agents to all 90,000 employees starting August, a significant corporate AI adoption. These agents will manage tasks and optimize model selection for efficiency and cost. The company is also investing in upskilling and sees AI as a major growth opportunity, with AI-related orders surging. Despite the potential, Cisco acknowledges the adoption process can be challenging.

title: 'Eetha': Shraddha Kapoor triples her paycheque to Rs 15 crore for biopic
content: Shraddha Kapoor has reportedly hiked her acting fee to a whopping Rs. 15 crore for her upcoming 2026 film 'Eetha', a biographical drama directed by Laxman Utekar. Based on the life of legendary Marathi Tamasha artist Vithabai Narayangaonkar, Eetha marks a massive salary jump for the actress following the historic success of Stree 2. Backed by producer Dinesh Vijan, the film is scheduled for a theatrical release on August 28, 2026, amid an ongoing title controversy raised by the Nationalist Congress Party (NCP) and Vithabai’s surviving family members.

title: 'Main na thodi funny type ki hoon': Dentist who mocked Ketan's death apologises
content: A dentist, Muskan Soni, has publicly apologized after facing severe backlash for mocking the death of Pune realtor Ketan Agrawal. Soni attributed her actions to a "funny personality" and claimed no intent to offend. The apology follows her five-year suspension from the All India Dental Students and Surgeons Association due to her "inappropriate and disrespectful remarks." Agrawal's death is being investigated as a murder, with two individuals arrested.

title: Sarpanches in Rajasthan, MP linked to trafficking of children
content: An organized child trafficking network has been uncovered in Ahmedabad, with children being recruited from impoverished families in Rajasthan and Madhya Pradesh. Village chiefs allegedly act as brokers, facilitating the movement of children for begging at lucrative spots like temples and traffic signals. Authorities have identified 276 trafficked children and are preparing criminal cases against exploiters, aiming to rehabilitate the victims.

title: NET Sociology paper leaves candidates lost in typos
content: Sociology students are voicing their discontent with the latest UGC-NET exam, pointing out critical spelling mistakes, incorrect names of well-known theorists, and awkward translations in Hindi. Errors like confusing 'Ritzer' with 'Putzer' and translating 'social' as 'oval' have raised red flags. Many candidates suspect that certain questions seemed AI-generated, straying from the established syllabus and hindering their understanding during the exam.

title: India return to SL for first Test tour since Kohli's 3-0 sweep; check full schedule
content: The first Test will be played at the Galle International Cricket Stadium in Galle from August 15 to 19. The second Test will take place at the Singhalese Sports Club in Colombo from August 23 to 27.

title: WhatsApp usernames decoded: Your questions answered, government's concerns explained
content: India's government has urged Meta to halt WhatsApp usernames, citing concerns over potential impersonation and cybercrime. While Meta claims usernames enhance privacy by obscuring phone numbers, officials fear it could aid bad actors. The feature allows users to create unique handles, but the government seeks assurances on its security before widespread adoption. This move could significantly impact how users interact and protect their digital identities.

title: IIT alumnus says AI can't replace the power of the right peer group
content: As AI makes knowledge more accessible than ever, entrepreneur Shikhar Agrawal revisits the value of his IIT Bombay education. He argues that the institution's greatest strength was never its degree or brand name, but the ambitious peer network that shaped his thinking. In the AI era, he believes such transformative learning environments remain difficult to replicate.

title: Kangana Slams Dheeraj Dhoopar: Calls out Shah Rukh claim on Lock Upp; promo sparks fan reactions
content: Kangana Ranaut's return to Lock Upp: Sach Ya Saza has injected fresh drama, with the host sharply critiquing contestants. She notably dismissed Dheeraj Dhoopar's comparisons to Shah Rukh Khan, questioning his 'main character energy.' Kangana also confronted Ram about his approach to the game, advising him against defensiveness. The show, featuring a diverse cast, promises more excitement with Kangana's presence.

title: From grief support to marriage prospects: How AI is transforming life in South Korea
content: South Korea is rapidly integrating AI and robotics into daily life, from recreating deceased loved ones to powering unstaffed businesses. This surge is driven by an aging population and a national strategy to become a global AI leader, with massive investments in chips and robotics. The AI boom is also reshaping careers, making semiconductor engineers highly sought-after and influencing educational choices.

title: 7 killed, 6 injured as boulders cave in at quarry near Bengaluru
content: A devastating rockfall at a quarry in Ramanagara claimed the lives of seven workers and injured at least six others. The tragedy occurred at Cauvery Crushers when loose boulders collapsed on a group of around 20 men. Families of the deceased, who were migrant workers seeking daily wages, are left heartbroken. Authorities have pledged action against illegal quarrying activities.

title: Balogun joins Zidane, Ronaldinho in unwanted World Cup history after red card vs Bosnia
content: USA secured a spot in the World Cup 2026 Round of 16 with a 2-0 win over Bosnia-Herzegovina. However, striker Folarin Balogun made unwanted history, becoming the fourth player to score and get sent off in a knockout match. Despite his red card for an accidental foul, the US team held on for the victory, but Balogun's suspension creates a selection headache for their next game against Belgium.

title: Tattoo on right forearm costs two CRPF inspectors promotion; Delhi HC dismisses pleas
content: Two CRPF inspectors were denied promotion to Assistant Commandant due to tattoos on their right forearms, deemed the saluting limb. The Delhi High Court upheld the decision, clarifying that while tattoos aren't banned, strict rules govern their placement, size, and content. The court ruled that having tattoos removed after being declared unfit doesn't qualify them for the current vacancies, advising them to reapply for future examinations.

title: Abhishek breaks KL Rahul's record in England, becomes fastest ever to...
content: India's first T20I against England was abandoned due to rain after the visitors posted 189/7. Abhishek Sharma starred with a record-breaking 59 off 24 balls, registering the fastest T20I fifty by an Indian in England, while captain Shreyas Iyer scored 68. Shivam Dube's unbeaten 42 provided a late flourish before persistent rain forced a no-result.

title: Ram temple donation row: How counting room key became central to theft probe
content: Ayodhya police are intensifying their probe into the Ram Temple donation theft, planning to question accused Ramshankar Yadav, alias Tinnu, in jail. Fresh disclosures suggest Tinnu had a key to the donation counting room, potentially enabling him to manipulate cash offerings. Investigators are also examining claims of unrestricted access to administrative areas due to proximity to a former trustee, and how accused coordinated movements to evade CCTV surveillance during counting.

title: Fresh violence in Manipur as 20 houses in 3 villages set ablaze
content: Fresh violence erupted in Manipur's Kamjong district as unidentified attackers torched over 20 houses across three villages. Phaimol, a Kuki village, was set ablaze, followed by suspected retaliatory attacks on Tangkhul Naga villages. While security forces intervened, several structures were damaged. Investigations are underway to identify the perpetrators, with a Kuki group condemning the attacks and alleging involvement of armed cadres.

title: 'Welcome to the Jungle' BO day 6: Movie eyes Rs 90 crore in week 1 finish
content: Ahmed Khan's 'Welcome to the Jungle', starring Akshay Kumar, Suniel Shetty and Paresh Rawal, is set to wrap up its opening week on Day 7 with domestic net collections poised to cross the Rs 90 crore mark. After a slower Day 6 that saw the film dip to Rs 6.15 crore, the multi-starrer comedy has pushed its total India net past Rs 87 crore. The film's worldwide gross has crossed Rs 129 crore, and with 'Alpha' starring Alia Bhatt, Bobby Deol and Sharvari opening Friday.

title: Tamil Nadu govt moves SC against Madras HC order banning cow slaughter
content: Tamil Nadu has approached the Supreme Court challenging the Madras High Court's sweeping ban on cow and calf slaughter. The state argues the High Court's order, based on a 1976 ruling, contradicts the Tamil Nadu Animal Preservation Act, 1958. This Act permits the slaughter of older, unfit cows under specific conditions, a provision the state contends was ignored by the blanket prohibition.

title: Goa cabinet approves 'Mhajo Flat' scheme to resolve long-pending disputes
content: Goa's cabinet has approved the 'Mhajo Flat' initiative, providing essential support for countless flat owners embroiled in longstanding ownership disputes. This program empowers residents to establish cooperative societies, enabling them to secure legal ownership of their homes, even amid builder absence or landowner conflicts. With a minimal fee and stamp duty, this move facilitates rightful ownership and encourages the revitalization of aging properties.

title: India-bound oil tanker hit by shrapnel near Hormuz reaches Odisha port safely
content: A crude oil tanker, the MT Sanmar Herald, has successfully docked at Odisha's Paradip Port after a perilous journey from Iraq. The vessel, carrying valuable crude oil, endured shrapnel damage to its bridge during hostilities in the Middle East. Despite facing prolonged delays and navigating a reopened Strait of Hormuz, the ship and its entire crew arrived safely, showcasing remarkable resilience and professionalism.

title: B'luru daycare incident: Staffer who had reported abuse of toddlers was fired
content: Disturbing videos alleging child abuse at a Bengaluru daycare center have led to the arrest of five nannies. The facility, located on Capgemini's campus, reportedly subjected toddlers to mistreatment, including being placed in a washing machine and locked in toilets. An official who previously reported the abuse was allegedly fired. Police are investigating the videos and other evidence, vowing to hold all involved accountable.

title: Who should file ITR & is income tax return filing mandatory for salaried taxpayers?
content: The important thing to understand is that ITR filing is a statutory requirement and an important financial record-keeping. For the financial year 2025-26, the basic exemption limit for individuals below 60 years of age is Rs 2.5 lakh under the old income tax regime and Rs 4 lakh under the new income tax regime.

title: 'Que sera, sera': Overlooked in Punjab Cong revamp, Manish Tewari posts cryptic message
content: Congress MP Manish Tewari expressed his disappointment after being overlooked for new roles in Punjab's election committees. He alluded to "insecurities" hindering talented individuals. Despite this, Tewari acknowledged the party's long-standing support and his own dedication. The appointments, announced by the AICC, saw other prominent leaders like Charanjit Singh Channi and Vijay Inder Singla given key positions for the upcoming 2027 Assembly elections.

title: Thailand: 11-year-old crashes truck into group of pilgrims; 8 monks killed
content: Tragedy struck in northeastern Thailand as at least eight monks on a pilgrimage were killed when an 11-year-old boy lost control of a pickup truck. The vehicle veered off the road, striking the group. Five monks died at the scene, with three more succumbing to their injuries at a hospital. Fourteen others were hospitalized following the devastating incident. Authorities are investigating the cause.

title: Watch: Yashasvi Jaiswal flaunts 'Messi' jersey during France vs Sweden WC match
content: Dreams do come true! Cricket star Yashasvi Jaiswal celebrated a major milestone by attending the exciting France vs. Sweden FIFA match in New York, wearing an Argentina jersey, in honor of Lionel Messi. This appearance arrives as he takes a breather following an unusual absence from the most recent ODI selections, with eyes set on rejoining the Test series against New Zealand.

title: FIFA WC Golden Boot: Kane draws level with Haaland, closes in on Messi & Mbappé
content: Harry Kane's stunning late brace against Congo DR has ignited the FIFA World Cup 2026 Golden Boot race, propelling him to five goals, just one behind leaders Messi and Mbappé.  Kane's heroics secured England's comeback win and kept their World Cup dreams alive, setting up an enthralling battle for the top scorer title as the knockout stages commence. He also surpassed Pelé on the all-time World Cup scoring list.

title: Fired for taking tea, biscuits from office: Jharkhand HC orders peon’s reinstatement
content: A Jharkhand court has ordered the reinstatement of a peon sacked for taking office tea and biscuits home. The court deemed the dismissal "grossly disproportionate," highlighting a vague show-cause notice and the lack of consideration for his plea. The bench emphasized that such a harsh penalty for a low-paid employee was insensitive, directing his re-employment with partial back wages.

title: '1st he cheats, then calls': Old clip shows Siya in heated call at nightclub
content: An old video of Siya Goyal, accused in her fiancé Ketan Agarwal's murder, has resurfaced, showing her in a nightclub using abusive language. Another clip shows her at a family function weeks before the alleged crime. Investigators are now scrutinizing Goyal's handling of Agarwal's mobile phone post-death, suspecting evidence tampering. The couple allegedly planned the murder, with a crime scene recreation underway.

title: Monsoon arrives in India: Heavy rain lashes Delhi, Mumbai; to spread to northern, western states
content: Delhi finally welcomed the southwest monsoon on Thursday, five days past its usual arrival, bringing much-needed relief from heat. The monsoon is now widespread across northwest India, with heavy rains expected in Maharashtra, Goa, and Gujarat. This marks the first July onset for Delhi since 2021, as the monsoon rapidly advances, nearing full country coverage ahead of schedule.

title: New EPF scheme : What it means for your provident fund savings
content: India’s provident fund framework has now entered a new phase with the notification of the Employees’ Provident Funds Scheme, 2026 (EPF Scheme, 2026) under the Code on Social Security, 2020.

title: Digvijaya Singh's 'Fact-Check' Triggers Congress Meltdown, Draws Rare BJP Praise
content: Digvijaya Singh, for decades one of the BJP's favourite targets, is suddenly being praised by ruling party leaders in Madhya Pradesh.

title: Man Missing After Falling Into Manhole Amid Rain In Mumbai, Rescue Ops On
content: The man's umbrella and slippers were found at the spot, and a search operation involving the Mumbai Fire Brigade and the police has been launched to trace him.

title: Political Row Erupts As K Kavitha Arrested During Land Protest In Telangana
content: The protesters demanded that the Revanth Reddy-led Congress government fulfil its poll promise of providing 250 square yards of land to Telangana movement activists

title: India-Japan Summit 2026 LIVE: Japan, India Working Together To Promote Industry In Northeast: Piyush Goyal
content: India-Japan Summit 2026 LIVE News Updates: The event today will feature prominent voices including Union Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal, Daikin India Chairman and Managing Director Kanwal Jeet Jawa, India's Ambassador to Japan Nagma Mallick and many others

title: Who Is Vadym Yermolaiev? Sanctioned Ukrainian Tycoon Wounded In Monaco Parcel Bomb Attack
content: Kyiv alleges the 58-year-old maintained an alcohol business in annexed Crimea -- paying taxes to Russia even after it invaded Ukraine.

title: TVK-Led Secular Alliance Is Confident As Allies Eye Long-Term Partnership
content: Chief Minister Vijay hosted leaders and legislators of parties supporting his minority government at a tea meeting in a star hotel on the outskirts of Chennai.

title: TikTok Settles Social Media Addiction Lawsuit With US Teen Ahead Of Trial
content: Accused of using features like infinite scroll to hook kids, TikTok chose to settle, following a previous $27 million school district payout.

title: Adani Group Sees Odisha As Heart Of Eastern India's Economic Growth: Karan Adani
content: Addressing the gathering, Karan Adani said that Adani Group Chairman, Gautam Adani, "sees in Odisha not just resources, but resilience".

title: Jagan Reddy's 'Mavigan' Pitch For Next Election Renews Andhra Capital Battle
content: YS Jagan also renewed his attack on the TDP-led NDA government, blaming it for using Amaravati as a cover for corruption.

title: ShareChat, India's Meta Rival, Targets $400 Million IPO Next Year
content: The profit and the planned listing mark a significant reversal for the firm that spent the past few years cutting costs and laying off staff as venture funding dried up after the Covid pandemic.

title: Piyush Goyal Recalls "Nightmare" His Team Faced During US-India Trade Talks
content: Goyal noted that all the different elements -- whether it's the concessions in different goods or breaking down non-tariff barriers-- have been largely finalised.

title: Stock Market LIVE Updates, Sensex Today: Sensex Jumps 400 Points Higher As Crude Oil Eases Further
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Brent crude falls 1.09% to $70.79 while US WTI falls 1.27% to $67.71 per barrel.

title: 8 Thai Monks Killed, 10 Injured As Child Drives Mini Truck Into Procession
content: Police said the boy had taken his parents' pickup truck without permission before losing control of the vehicle and crashing into the monks.

title: "Have A Funny Personality": Dentist Who Mocked Ketan Agarwal Apologises
content: Dentist Muskan Soni pointed to the abuse coming her way, forcing her to switch her YouTube account to private.

title: "To Avoid Challans": Truth Behind Cheap Passenger Helmets In Bike Taxi Rides
content: Several riders said the helmet is only meant to show traffic police that the passenger is wearing one and not for safety.

title: Only 1.7 Lakh Of 8.7 Crore Indian MSMEs Export. What's Stopping The Rest?
content: The government has been pushing exports through initiatives such as Make in India, PLI schemes and a growing network of Free Trade Agreements.

title: Your Phone Says 'No Service.' Minutes Later, Your Savings Could Be Gone
content: Victims realise they've been attacked only after they receive emails about successful fund transfers or discover their accounts have been emptied.

title: Kashmir Faces Mutton Crisis Amid Wedding Season Over Punjab 'Transit Fee' Row
content: Mutton dealers allege the contractors on Punjab highways impose a transit fee of Rs 20,000-25,000 on each truck carrying the livestock.

title: US Lifts Export Ban On Anthropic's Fable 5, Gives Limited Access To Mythos 5
content: The US government lifted restrictions on Anthropic's advanced AI models, resolving a safety and political standoff regarding the technology.
